body.title: DickSimnel: true CC parity — sprint-ticket protocol, DONE: gate, turn-1 tool enforcement
body.result: stripped bespoke Analysis/Confidence format; DONE: gate + turn-1 correction in ToolLoop; sprint-ticket skill is now sole procedural guide
body.description:
DickSimnel must be a CC mirror — the only difference is cost/provider (inference proxy handles that). A Claude Code expert should find zero behavioral differences.

1. Strip the bespoke `## Analysis / ## Implementation / ## Tests / ## Confidence` format block from `SYSTEM_PROMPT` in device.py — it instructs the OR model to plan and stop, which is exactly the fake-DONE pattern. The sprint-ticket skill (already loaded via `_build_system_prompt`) is the sole procedural guide.
2. `ToolLoop.run()`: if turn 1 returns no tool_calls, inject an assistant-correction message ('You must call a tool first — Read the affected files now') and continue. Never return turn-1 planning text.
3. `_post_result()`: only close when result starts with `DONE:` AND `git log --oneline -1` shows a commit for the ticket ID since work started. Anything else → escalate to CC with full result as context.

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/device.py, devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; OOP-first; log every state change
**Scope boundary:** DickSimnel sprint protocol only; inference proxy routing and caching are separate tickets
**Completion criteria:** Re-run T-web-ui-circuit-breakers through DickSimnel; it must produce a git commit with real code changes and passing tests before closing. Planning-text result → escalate, not close.
